studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Measurements were collected at baseline, 3 months, 6 months (primary endpoint), 9 months, and 12 months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received immediate 12-month access to the web-based intervention (Optimune) in addition to care as usual (CAU). The control group received CAU and access to Optimune after a 6-month delay (waitlist control).
studies.collected_data: Primary outcomes: Plasma concentrations of CRP, IL-6, TNF-α, stimulated IL-6, and stimulated TNF-α. Secondary outcomes: Plasma concentrations of various cytokines (e.g., IL-1β, IFN-α, MCP-1), concentrations of secreted cytokines after stimulation (e.g., PMA, LPS), circulating numbers of lymphocytes, monocytes, granulocytes, phenotypic analysis of T and NK cell subsets, cortisol awakening response (CAR), cancer-related fatigue (BFI-9), emotional stress (IES-R), depression (PHQ-9), anxiety (GAD-7), fear of progression (PA-F12), program usefulness, negative effects (INEP), recurrence of breast cancer, frequency of common cold or flu, and frequency of unscheduled medical encounters.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Measurements were collected at baseline, 3 months (primary endpoint), and 6 months post-baseline.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group received immediate 12-month access to the web-based intervention (Optimune) and could also use care as usual (CAU). The control group received CAU and access to Optimune after a 3-month delay (waitlist control).
studies.collected_data: WHOQOL-BREF (Quality of Life), International Physical Activity Questionnaire (IPAQ - short), Food Quality Questionnaire (FQQ), Brief Fatigue Inventory Questionnaire (BFI-9), Impact of Event Scale (IES-R), Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9), Generalized Anxiety Disorder questionnaire (GAD-7), PA-F12 (Fear of Progression), Insomnia Severity Index (ISI), questionnaire on program usefulness, Inventory for the Assessment of Negative Effects of Psychotherapy (INEP), questionnaire on recurrence of breast cancer, questionnaire on frequency of common cold or virus flu, questionnaire on frequency of unscheduled medical encounters, questionnaire on Body Mass Index, questionnaire on psychotherapy initiation.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Follow-up surveys are still ongoing despite the study being discontinued.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: access to the online program optimune for 365 days in addition to routine treatment (care as usual). Control group: routine treatment (care as usual), access to the online program optimune for 365 days after completion of the study.
studies.collected_data: Primary Outcome: Quality of life (total score Assessment of Quality of Life; AQoL-8D) at T1 (3 months after baseline). Secondary Outcomes: General distress (Depression-Anxiety-Stress-Scales, DASS-21), Work and social ability (Work and Social Adjustment Scale, WSAS), Fatigue (Brief Fatigue Inventory, BFI), responder rate of AQoL-8D, subscales and dimensions of AQoL-8D.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Participants in the control group received the intervention after a delay of 3 months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received care as usual (CAU) plus 12-month access to Optimune immediately after randomization. Control group received CAU and Optimune after a delay of 3 months.
studies.collected_data: Quality of life (QoL), physical activity, dietary habits.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Significant effects on QoL (d = 0.27, 95% CI: 0.07-0.48) and dietary habits (d = 0.36, 95% CI: 0.15-0.56). Effect on physical exercise was not significant (d = 0.30; 95% CI: 0.10-0.51).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary endpoints were assessed at three months.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received care as usual (CAU) plus 12-month access to Optimune immediately after randomization. Control group received CAU and Optimune after a delay of 3 months.
studies.collected_data: Quality of life (QoL), physical activity, dietary habits.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Significant effects on QoL (d = 0.27, 95% CI: 0.07–0.48) and dietary habits (d = 0.36, 95% CI: 0.15–0.56). Effect on physical exercise was not significant (d = 0.30; 95% CI: 0.10–0.51).
